kpi_window_note: All % changes in this row are typically one-month (~30 calendar days); IBIT and ETHA may use one-year figures when one-month Yahoo data is unavailable. Aggregate AUM % uses estimated weekly series. Fund-flow % compares 30-day Farside spot BTC/ETH ETF totals vs the prior 30 days. Dollar totals from StockAnalysis.
key_observations_html:
• U.S. ETP 30d net flow rose 203.2% over 30D: Listed-product flows printing +$1.66B (203.2% over 30D) indicate investors are still using ETPs as the primary regulated gateway into crypto beta—supporting demand even when spot prices are choppy. Related: Circle soars after securing U.S. trust bank approval in crypto expansion (CoinDesk).
  • Forward market-size scenarios are large, but still assumptions-driven: Public analyst commentary continues to frame the next few years in a broad range—for example, one widely cited 2026 scenario set points to roughly $180B–$220B for Bitcoin ETF assets, compared with about $75.92B across U.S. spot Bitcoin ETPs on this page today (see ). Treat these as directional planning ranges, not a base-case forecast.
  • Regulatory framing still decides product access: Approval and listing decisions set the feasible product set on U.S. exchanges, so issuers already in the fund table have a structural advantage when new access opens. Related: Circle's (CRCL) OCC approval fails to ease core concerns, Mizuho says (CoinDesk).
  • The launch pipeline is crowded: This page lists 172 U.S. crypto ETPs; industry filing trackers and analyst snapshots still cite 120+ crypto ETP filings in the U.S. queue (example: ). Filing volume does not mean all products launch or scale, but it does point to a heavier near-term slate of spot and spot-adjacent product attempts.

Context for strategy only (not investment advice). Bullets rank listed fund data, Farside flow KPIs, and recent ETF/ETP headlines on this page. Bullets combine on-page data with recent industry headlines.

Key observations are AI generated and should be reviewed for accuracy.
